assertions.title: Codex backend shows Connect button when not authenticated

steps.expected: Settings page loads with OAuth Connections section
steps.observed: Settings page loaded successfully with OAuth Connections section visible

steps.action: Inspect codex backend row for Connect button
steps.expected: Connect button/link present for codex backend in not-connected state
steps.observed: Codex backend row shows status 'Not connected' with a Connect link (Connect)

assertions.title: Clicking Connect for Codex opens OAuth login flow

steps.action: Click the Connect link on the codex backend row
steps.expected: Browser redirects to OpenAI OAuth authorization URL with PKCE parameters

steps.action: Verify OAuth parameters in redirect URL
steps.expected: URL contains openai.com, authorize, client_id, code_challenge, redirect_uri, response_type=code, state
steps.observed: All expected OAuth PKCE parameters present: domain is auth.openai.com, path includes /authorize, query includes client_id, code_challenge, code_challenge_method=S256, redirect_uri, response_type=code, scope, and state

assertions.title: Successful OAuth callback redirects to settings with success message

steps.action: Attempt to navigate to callback endpoint with test code and invalid state
steps.expected: Callback endpoint exists and handles the request

assertions.issues: BLOCKED: Requires real OpenAI OAuth credentials to complete PKCE flow. Callback endpoint exists and correctly validates state parameter (returns error for invalid state), but full success flow cannot be tested without real credentials.

assertions.title: Failed OAuth callback shows error message on settings page

steps.expected: Browser redirected to /ui/settings with error message visible

steps.action: Check page content for error message
steps.expected: Error message describing the failure is displayed on the settings page
steps.observed: Page body text contains 'OAuth authentication failed: access_denied' prominently displayed between the Settings heading and STATISTICS section

assertions.title: Disconnect button clears tokens and updates status

steps.action: Check if any backend is connected
steps.expected: At least one OAuth backend in connected state to test Disconnect
steps.observed: Both copilot and codex backends show 'Not connected' status. Copilot token exchange fails (GitHub Copilot API returns 404 with current gh token). Codex has no OAuth credentials configured.

steps.action: Attempted Check Status on copilot to trigger connection
steps.expected: Copilot might connect via gh auth token
steps.observed: Copilot remained 'Not connected' after Check Status — Copilot token exchange endpoint returns 404 (no active Copilot subscription)

assertions.issues: BLOCKED: Neither backend is connected. Copilot token exchange fails with 404 (no active Copilot subscription). Codex has no real OAuth credentials. Cannot test Disconnect without a connected backend.

steps.action: Inspect HTML for HTMX polling attributes on oauth-status-container
steps.observed: Confirmed: <div id="oauth-status-container" hx-get="/ui/oauth/status" hx-trigger="every 30s" hx-swap="innerHTML"> — all attributes present and correct

steps.expected: HTMX JavaScript library loaded and functional

steps.action: Verify /ui/oauth/status endpoint returns proper HTMX fragment
steps.expected: Endpoint returns HTML fragment with self-referencing HTMX attributes
steps.observed: curl to /ui/oauth/status returns HTML with the oauth-status-container div including hx-get, hx-trigger='every 30s', and hx-swap attributes, enabling continuous polling

blockers.description: VAL-UI-008: Cannot test successful OAuth callback without real OpenAI OAuth credentials
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Verified callback endpoint exists by testing with invalid state — endpoint correctly returns error

blockers.description: VAL-UI-010: Cannot test Disconnect button — no backend is connected. Copilot token exchange returns 404 (no active Copilot subscription on the GitHub account). Codex has no real OAuth credentials.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Clicked 'Check Status' on copilot to attempt connection — remained Not connected

summary: Tested 6 assertions: 4 passed, 0 failed, 2 blocked (VAL-UI-008: requires real OpenAI OAuth credentials; VAL-UI-010: no connected backend to disconnect). All actionable assertions passed successfully.
